# ClusterOS
> Diagnostic intelligence infrastructure for regional innovation economies.

ClusterOS identifies why regional innovation clusters stall — behavioural patterns where individually rational actor choices combine to prevent systemic progress. It maps the stabilisation stacks those patterns form, and delivers specific leverage hypotheses: testable perturbations calibrated to the stack.

Built by Andrew Barrie (co-founder, Barrie & Hibbert, acquired by Moody's Analytics 2011 for $77.6M). Headquartered in Edinburgh. Operated by Community Lab.

## What ClusterOS produces

- **Stall identification**: which of 9 canonical stall types are active, with X-side (observable) and Y-side (what doesn't happen) evidence
- **Stack analysis**: the mutually reinforcing stall configuration and why single interventions fail
- **Structural resemblances**: 2–3 comparable ecosystems from the diagnostic database with similar configurations
- **Leverage hypotheses**: testable perturbations with defined success metrics and explicit confidence levels

## The 9 canonical stalls

- S1: Re-proving instead of narrowing (every pathway shows promise; none becomes dominant)
- S2: Coordinating instead of deciding (97% frequency — most common stall identified)
- S3: Forgiving instead of redesigning (underperforming actors preserved; relationships maintained)
- S4: Extracting without reinvesting (talent, capital, credibility flows out; nothing channels it back)
- S5: Mediating instead of coupling (intermediaries prevent direct connection)
- S6: Stabilising around incumbents (new ideas assessed by whether they complement existing trajectory)
- S7: Narrating instead of testing (91% frequency — confidence grows through articulation, not exposure)
- S8: Scaling activity instead of throughput (88% frequency — what can be counted expands; what compounds is harder to trace)
- S9: Waiting for permission (initiative deferred to endorsement)

## Evidence base

- 75 clusters diagnosed
- 18+ countries and regions
- 50–170 verified evidence items per cluster
- Geographies: North America, Europe, Middle East, Southeast Asia, Australasia
- Sectors: cyber security, advanced manufacturing, space technology, medtech, digital media, agtech, photonics, simulation & training, regional innovation

## Pricing

- **Cluster Snapshot**: £5,000 fixed fee · 4–5 weeks · public data pipeline + actor questionnaire · stall identification, stack analysis, structural resemblances, leverage hypotheses
- **Collaborative Diagnostic**: scoped pricing · your data + working sessions + anchor interviews · board-ready output

## Architecture (for technical readers)

ClusterOS runs on a federated MCP (Model Context Protocol) architecture:
- Each regional EDA operates a sovereign, isolated database instance
- Data does not leave the regional tenant; no cross-region model training without consent
- The AI reasoning engine calls named MCP tools at runtime: get_actor_profile(), detect_stalls(), get_matched_connections(), propagate_signal(), get_steward_diagnostic(), get_leverage_hypotheses(), search_support_programmes(), ingest_evidence()
- Autonomous bot network ingests external signals continuously (government support directories, community service APIs, publication databases, professional identity systems)
- Cold-start solved: platform knows the ecosystem before any actor joins

## Cluster profiles

Individual cluster diagnostic profiles are available at: https://clusteros.io/clusters/{cluster-id}.html
Example: https://clusteros.io/clusters/belfast-cyber-security.html
